@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2m0","title":"Onboarding polish: lesson 8 visibility + tailscale jq-optional + update warn","description":"Land small UX/robustness tweaks: ensure onboarding menus/progress account for lesson 8 (Keeping Updated); make tailscale status parsing work without jq; add warn-level output helper to update logger; and keep stack installers from forcing --yes in interactive mode.","acceptance_criteria":"- Onboard menus/progress include lesson 8 (total 9 lessons)\n- tailscale.sh parses BackendState without jq installed\n- update.sh supports warn log type\n- stack.sh only adds --yes flags when non-interactive","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:07:43.806195-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","close_reason":"Updated onboarding to include lesson 8; added jq-optional tailscale BackendState parsing; added warn log helper; and made stack installers respect interactive mode."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","title":"[ubu.2] Extend state.sh for upgrade tracking","description":"# Extend state.sh for Upgrade Tracking ## Purpose Add upgrade-specific state fields to track multi-reboot upgrade progress. ## State Schema Extensions Add new fields to state.json schema: ```json { "schema_version": 3, // Bump version for new fields // ... existing fields ... // NEW: Ubuntu upgrade tracking "ubuntu_upgrade": { "enabled": true, "started_at": "2025-12-21T10:00:00Z", "original_version": "24.04", "target_version": "25.10", "current_stage": "upgrading", "upgrade_path": ["24.04", "24.10", "25.04", "25.10"], "completed_upgrades": [ {"from": "24.04", "to": "24.10", "completed_at": "..."} ], "current_upgrade": {"from": "24.10", "to": "25.04", "started_at": "..."}, "needs_reboot": false, "resume_after_reboot": true, "last_error": null } } ``` ## Functions to Add ```bash # Initialize upgrade state state_upgrade_init() { local original_version="$1" local target_version="$2" local upgrade_path="$3" # JSON array } # Mark current upgrade as started state_upgrade_start() { local from_version="$1" local to_version="$2" } # Mark current upgrade as completed state_upgrade_complete() { local to_version="$1" } # Mark that system needs reboot state_upgrade_needs_reboot() { # Sets needs_reboot=true, resume_after_reboot=true } # Clear reboot flags after successful resume state_upgrade_resumed() { # Sets needs_reboot=false } # Check if upgrade is complete state_upgrade_is_complete() { # Returns 0 if current version >= target version } # Get current upgrade stage state_upgrade_get_stage() { # Returns: not_started | upgrading | awaiting_reboot | completed } # Record upgrade error state_upgrade_set_error() { local error_msg="$1" } # Clean up upgrade state after completion state_upgrade_cleanup() { # Remove ubuntu_upgrade section entirely } ``` ## Schema Migration Add migration from v2 to v3: - v3 is backwards compatible (new fields are optional) - Existing v2 state files work without modification - ubuntu_upgrade section only added when upgrade starts ## Why Separate State Section? The upgrade state is conceptually different from installation state: - Upgrade happens BEFORE installation - Upgrade may span multiple reboots - Upgrade state should be cleaned up after completion Keeping it in a separate section makes this clear and allows independent cleanup. ## Atomic Write Considerations All state writes already use atomic temp-file+rename pattern. This is critical for upgrade state since we're about to reboot and can't risk corrupted state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:20:15.998308-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","close_reason":"Extended state.sh with 14 upgrade tracking functions (state_upgrade_init, state_upgrade_start, state_upgrade_complete, etc.). Updated schema to v3 with backward compatibility for v2.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:35.258019-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yzm","title":"Enhance SSH disconnection recovery with prominent AlertCard","description":"# Task: Enhance SSH Disconnection Recovery Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu) ## Current State run-installer/page.tsx already has: - GuideCaution: "Do not close the terminal window while installation is running" - GuideTip: "If your internet connection drops, just SSH back in and run again" However, this is buried in the SimplerGuide. A more prominent AlertCard near the install command would be more reassuring. ## Enhancement Add a prominent AlertCard near the install command: ```tsx

Do not panic! If your SSH connection drops during installation:

  1. The installer keeps running on the VPS
  2. Just SSH back in using the same command
  3. Run the installer command again — it will resume where it left off

The installer is designed to be run multiple times safely. If anything fails, you can always re-run it.

``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Prominent AlertCard near the install command (not buried in SimplerGuide) - [ ] Explicitly says "do not panic" - [ ] Explains installer keeps running - [ ] Explains safe to re-run (idempotent) - [ ] Reassuring tone with info variant (blue/calming) ## Note This elevates existing content from SimplerGuide to a more visible location.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:20.981302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","close_reason":"Added prominent AlertCard with Wifi icon near install command explaining connection drop recovery: (1) installer keeps running, (2) SSH back in, (3) re-run safely"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","title":"EPIC: UX Audit Implementation - Round 1 & 2 Recommendations","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:41:41.025777-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:12:16.565061-05:00","comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented small UX fix: updated tmux detach shortcut in /wizard/launch-onboarding from Ctrl+B then D to Ctrl+A then D (matches ACFS tmux.conf). Ran apps/web lint + type-check successfully.","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:33Z"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","title":"EPIC: UX Audit Implementation - Round 1 & 2 Recommendations","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:41:41.025777-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:12:16.565061-05:00","comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented small UX fix: updated tmux detach shortcut in /wizard/launch-onboarding from Ctrl+B then D to Ctrl+A then D (matches ACFS tmux.conf). Ran apps/web lint + type-check successfully.","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:33Z"},{"id":9,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-314q","author":"jemanuel","text":"Added a reassurance block on Status Check to reduce auth fatigue (you don’t need to log into every service now). File: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx.","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:29:44Z"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-31e","title":"Installer: implement (or de-scope) cloud/db installs + skip flags","description":"install.sh defines --skip-postgres/--skip-vault/--skip-cloud and smoke test references them, but install.sh never installs PostgreSQL/Vault/cloud CLIs. scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh + acfs.manifest.yaml define these installs, and README claims they're installed.","acceptance_criteria":"Either: - installer runs cloud/db phase (respecting skip flags) without breaking Ubuntu 24.04/25.x CI, OR - docs/manifest updated to match reality and flags removed/hidden.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:17:29.066623-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cloud/db phase in install.sh (Postgres 18 via PGDG, Vault via HashiCorp repo, cloud CLIs via bun) honoring skip flags; updated smoke test + phase numbering."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","title":"Create 'Starting a New Project' guide section","description":"# Task: Create 'Starting a New Project' Guide Section ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc) ## Description The wizard uses "my-first-project" as an example, but doesn't explain the general pattern for starting real projects. Users need guidance on: - Creating project folders - Initializing git - Setting up with Claude ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx or new /learn/new-project page \`\`\`tsx

Starting a New Project

Ready to build something? Here's the pattern:

1. Create a session for your project

This creates a persistent workspace named "my-awesome-app".

2. Create and navigate to a project folder

3. Start Claude and describe your project

Tell Claude what you want to build. For example:

"Create a React app with TypeScript that shows a todo list"
Claude will set up the project structure, install dependencies, and start building. You can guide it step by step or give it the whole vision at once.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows 3-step pattern (session → folder → Claude) - [ ] Uses concrete example project - [ ] Includes example prompt for Claude - [ ] Explains what Claude will do - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Different color from daily workflow to distinguish - Example project should be relatable (todo app is classic) - Emphasize that Claude does the heavy lifting","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:00.569413-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:22:12.203033-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in launch-onboarding with: 3-step pattern (ntm new → mkdir cd → cc), example project 'my-awesome-app', example Claude prompt for React todo app, GuideTip explaining Claude's capabilities, cyan color scheme distinct from daily workflow.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-322g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s4cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:33.220094-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","title":"Implement agent auth functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement agent auth functional tests ## Context Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i) ## What to Do Create functional tests for AI agent authentication: ### check_claude_auth() 1. Check if claude binary exists (skip if not) 2. Check if ~/.claude/config.json exists 3. Try claude --print-system-info (low-cost API check) 4. Return: 0=OK, 1=fail, 2=skip ### check_codex_auth() 1. Check if codex binary exists 2. Check for OPENAI_API_KEY env var 3. Check for API key in ~/.zshrc.local 4. Return status ### check_gemini_auth() 1. Check if gemini binary exists 2. Check for GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY 3. Check for credentials file 4. Return status ## Acceptance Criteria - Each check has clear pass/fail criteria - Skipped if tool not installed (status 2) - Auth issues detected reliably - No false positives ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add check functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:18.598224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:57:34.018612-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented check_claude_auth(), check_codex_auth(), check_gemini_auth() with config/API key checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:34.958137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
